events.title: Drone strikes UAE nuclear plant highlighting risk of renewed war
events.description: A drone strike sparked a fire on the edge of the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant on Sunday in what authorities called an "unprovoked terrorist attack."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drone strikes UAE nuclear plant highlighting risk of renewed war

events.title: UN pleads for Equatorial Guinea not to send US asylum seekers to their home countries: ‘Their life would be in danger’
events.description: Human rights experts make rare public appeal as US deportees describe being held in ‘prison-like’ conditions Human rights experts at the United Nations issued a rare public appeal to Equatorial Guinea, urging the central African country to halt its plans to return US deportees to their home countries, where they face political violence, torture and death. The statement , co-signed by a representative of the African Commission on Human and People’s Rights, adds diplomatic pressure on Equatorial Guinea , one of the world’s most repressive regimes, to comply with international human rights standards and avoid refoulement, or the expulsion of people to countries where they face persecution. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump says Islamic State ‘second in command’ killed by US and Nigerian forces
events.description: US president calls Abu-Bilal al-Minuki ‘most active terrorist in the world’ and says he was eliminated in ‘very complex mission’ Donald Trump has said US and Nigerian forces killed the “second in command” global leader of the Islamic State. “Tonight, at my direction, brave American forces and the Armed Forces of Nigeria flawlessly executed a meticulously planned and very complex mission to eliminate the most active terrorist in the world from the battlefield,” the US president said on his Truth Social platform on Friday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump says Islamic State ‘second in command’ killed by US and Nigerian forces

events.title: Ebola outbreak in Central Africa declared a ‘Public Health Emergency of International Concern’
events.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern, citing rising cases, cross-border spread and significant uncertainties about the scale of the epidemic.

events.title: 1.1 billion people live in slums. Can they be housed in dignity?
events.description: Ministers, mayors, international organisations, urban planners and experts are in Baku, Azerbaijan, to tackle one of the world’s fastest-growing challenges: the global housing crisis, which, according to the United Nations, affects nearly 2.8 billion people worldwide.

events.title: Executions nearly doubled in the U.S. last year, and soared abroad
events.description: The number of people killed in state-sanctioned executions worldwide rose to a 44-year high in 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International.

events.title: Drones reshape war in Colombia as deaths and injuries mount
events.description: Civilians left increasingly exposed as a dangerous new front opens up in the country’s decades-old conflict As night fell over southern Colombia , and a group of children began their weekly Tuesday football match, a drone appeared overhead. The children looked up, and the drone dropped a grenade, its blast killing a 10-year-old boy and injuring 12 more civilians. The child’s death, in southern Cauca in 2024, marked the first known time a person in the country had been killed in a weaponised drone attack. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drones reshape war in Colombia as deaths and injuries mount

events.title: Ebola in Africa: Why it's a constant threat
events.description: Ebola is highly contagious and deadly. It spreads internationally, such as in the May 2026 outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. But that doesn't automatically make it a pandemic.

events.title: UAE nuclear power plant targeted in likely-Iranian attack
events.description: The UAE says a drone strike triggered a fire near a nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi. There were no reported casualties or radiological release from the strike, whose origin is currently unknown, but all eyes are on Iran. In the wake of the attack, US president Donald Trump is reportedly set to convene top national security advisers on May 18 to discuss military options.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UAE nuclear power plant targeted in likely-Iranian attack

events.title: Death sentences and executions on the rise worldwide
events.description: A record increase in the number of death sentences and executions worldwide in 2025. This is what Amnesty International’s recently published annual report reveals. As usual, China accounts for the highest number of cases, thought to be the world's lead executioner. But the humanitarian organisation also attributes this figure to Iran, where the number of executed nearly doubled compared to 2024.

events.title: Global energy and trade disruption pushing millions towards poverty
events.description: Disruptions to global energy supplies and trade corridors are driving up the cost of food, transport and essential goods worldwide, slowing economic growth and increasing pressure on vulnerable households and debt-strapped developing countries.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global energy and trade disruption pushing millions towards poverty

events.title: Executions worldwide hit highest level since 1981, Amnesty says
events.description: The human rights group Amnesty International confirmed that at least 2,707 people were put to death in 2025 but said the actual number was much higher. Despite these figures, there are indications of possible progress.

events.title: DR Congo takes emergency measures to contain new Ebola outbreak
events.description: DRC authorities have been advised to take emergency measures in a bid to contain the latest Ebola outbreak. Dozens of lives have already been claimed by the outbreak, which has been declared a public health emergency of international concern by the World health Organisation. Cases have also been reported in neighbouring Uganda, among travellers from Congo.

events.title: War against Iran is "growing more unpopular by the day" in the US
events.description: A drone strike close to a UAE nuclear plant raised concerns of an escalation in the conflict. Amid stalled talks, Trump is expected to meet national security advisers to discuss options for military action on May 19 and warned the 'clock is ticking' for Iran. But domestically, the US president also has to deal with a war that is growing 'more unpopular by the day', as FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani explains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War against Iran is "growing more unpopular by the day" in the US

events.title: Two dozen injured in Ukraine following Russian strikes
events.description: Russia targeted eight regions of Ukraine in its latest nighttime drone and missile barrage, with local authorities reporting that the strikes wounded more than two dozen civilians, including three children. The city of Dnipro bore the brunt of the attack, which saw more than 500 hundred drones launched against the central region of the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Two dozen injured in Ukraine following Russian strikes

events.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Central Africa a public health emergency after 80 suspected deaths
events.description: The WHO declared the Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda an international public health emergency after dozens of suspected deaths were reported.

events.title: UN welcomes $1.8 billion US boost for humanitarian operations
events.description: An additional $1.8 billion in US humanitarian funding will allow the United Nations and its partners to expand emergency relief operations reaching millions of people worldwide, as rising global needs and funding shortfalls force aid agencies to scale back assistance.

events.title: World Urban Forum opens in Baku as housing crisis and climate shocks intensify
events.description: Soaring housing costs, climate shocks and conflicts are leaving millions without adequate shelter – but what can be done? As the 13th UN World Urban Forum opens on Sunday in Baku, Azerbaijan, participants will grapple with solutions to a deepening global housing crisis.

events.title: At least eight people killed in Bangkok rail crossing collision
events.description: Thai authorities say 32 others injured near Makkasan station after freight train strikes bus and fire breaks out At least eight people were killed and 32 others were injured in Thailand after a freight train struck a bus at a rail crossing in Bangkok, rescue officials and a deputy transport minister said. Firefighters and rescue crews were dispatched as flames engulfed the bus and nearby vehicles close to the airport rail link’s Makkasan station, officials said, adding that the collision also involved cars and motorcycles. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ At least eight people killed in Bangkok rail crossing collision

events.title: World News in Brief: Malnutrition in Somalia, hunger crisis in DR Congo, energy shortages in Cuba
events.description: Nearly six million people in Somalia – almost a third of the population analysed – are projected to face acute hunger between April and June, with 1.9 million expected to experience emergency levels, UN-backed food security experts warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Malnutrition in Somalia, hunger crisis in DR Congo, energy shortages in Cuba

events.title: Iran war day 80: Trump issues warning; Tehran ‘ready’ to confront attacks
events.description: The fear of renewed US strikes in Iran looms while Israeli attacks continue in Lebanon despite extended 'ceasefire'.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war day 80: Trump issues warning; Tehran ‘ready’ to confront attacks

events.title: ‘Feels like an illusion’: inside post-Maduro Venezuela’s bewildering new era
events.description: Four months after Trump’s surprise raid, a political thaw has descended – but mingled with hope is trepidation for what comes next When Ángel Linares heard a strange buzz followed by an explosion, his first thought was that neighbours were setting off fireworks to celebrate the new year. Then his windows shattered, the building’s walls shook and its facade was ripped off, sending him flying on to the ground of an apartment suddenly reduced to rubble. His 85-year-old mother, Jesucita, feared Venezuela’s northern coast had been devastated by an earthquake, like the one she remembers from 1967. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘Feels like an illusion’: inside post-Maduro Venezuela’s bewildering new era

events.title: G7 finance ministers meet in Paris as headwinds mount for global economy
events.description: Finance ministers and central bankers from the world's top economies are gathering in Paris as the US war on Iran threatens to undermine the global financial system. Meanwhile, inflation tied to the war sparks a rout on bond markets, and the US's busiest commuter rail system shuts down over a labour dispute.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 G7 finance ministers meet in Paris as headwinds mount for global economy

events.title: Sudan hunger crisis deepens as UN warns millions face acute food shortages
events.description: Nearly 20 million people across Sudan are facing acute hunger and more than 800,000 children risk severe malnutrition this year, UN agencies warned on Friday, as civil war, mass displacement and collapsing food and health systems deepen one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises and push parts of the country closer to famine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan hunger crisis deepens as UN warns millions face acute food shortages

events.title: Lebanon accuses Iran of inserting IRGC terrorists into country 'under guise of diplomatic activity'
events.description: Lebanon reportedly filed a precedent-setting UN complaint against Iran amid U.S.-facilitated Israel-Lebanon normalization talks in Washington, D.C.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Lebanon accuses Iran of inserting IRGC terrorists into country 'under guise of diplomatic activity'

events.title: Could Ukraine's drone strikes mark a turning point in war with Russia?
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, May 18, 2026: First, Ukraine's drone attacks on Russia may mark a turning point in the war. Next, Venezuela's post-Maduro era is conflicting for locals. Also, there's more drama at the Cannes Film Festival as Canal+ hits back at filmmakers. Finally, Gen Z find new ways of enjoying themselves on a budget.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Could Ukraine's drone strikes mark a turning point in war with Russia?

events.title: At least 4 people killed in one of the largest Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia
events.description: At least four people, including three near Moscow, died in one of the largest Ukrainian overnight attacks against Russia since the start of the war, according to Russian local authorities
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At least 4 people killed in one of the largest Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia

events.title: Threatened indictment of Raúl Castro ratchets up US pressure on Cuba
events.description: Trump administration move echoes indictment of Venezuela’s Nicolás Maduro as fuel crisis racks Cuba Tensions between Cuba and US seem set to rise further amid reports that Raúl Castro, the country’s 94-year-old former president, may soon face the type of indictment that led to the US abduction of the Venezuelan leader, Nicolás Maduro, in January. Although Raúl is officially retired, he remains the most potent figure in Cuban politics following the death of his brother Fidel in 2016, and by targeting him Washington appears to be heaping pressure on Cuba’s communist leadership at the end of an already extraordinarily intense week. Continue reading...

events.title: Air Force intel vet allegedly spiraled into Iran’s terror underworld: Timeline of deceit, hunt for spy suspect
events.description: The FBI offers a $200,000 reward for tips on Monica Witt, a former U.S. Air Force intelligence officer wanted on espionage charges tied to Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Air Force intel vet allegedly spiraled into Iran’s terror underworld: Timeline of deceit, hunt for spy suspect

events.title: New Ebola outbreak leaves 65 dead as officials warn of cross-border spread
events.description: A new Ebola outbreak in Congo has killed 65 people with 246 suspected cases as officials investigate which strain is driving the deadly spread.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New Ebola outbreak leaves 65 dead as officials warn of cross-border spread

events.title: Nuclear terror threat ‘has never been so high’
events.description: The widespread availability of new technology, such as militarised drones and artificial intelligence, means that the current threat of nuclear terrorism is higher than it has ever been.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Nuclear terror threat ‘has never been so high’

events.title: Ebola outbreak kills 65 people in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo
events.description: Uganda also reports outbreak and health officials say cases were caused by Bundibugyo strain of virus An outbreak of Ebola has killed 65 people in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, health officials said. There have been 246 suspected cases of the haemorrhagic fever reported so far in the conflict-hit Ituri province, which shares borders with Uganda and South Sudan. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Ebola outbreak kills 65 people in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo

events.title: Italian minister says Modena attack raises integration concerns amid migration debate
events.description: Italy’s interior minister has said that a car-ramming and stabbing attack in the northern city of Modena cannot be dismissed as an isolated act, warning it highlights deeper challenges around integration and social distress
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Italian minister says Modena attack raises integration concerns amid migration debate

events.title: WHO sounds alarm over nicotine pouches targeting young people
events.description: Brightly coloured nicotine pouches promoted through social media influencers, music festivals and youth-oriented advertising are driving a rapid rise in nicotine use among young people worldwide, the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO sounds alarm over nicotine pouches targeting young people

events.title: Afghan mothers and children face worsening hunger crisis, WFP warns
events.description: Afghanistan’s deepening malnutrition crisis is pushing mothers and children to the brink, the UN World Food Programme has warned, as mass returns from neighbouring countries and severe funding shortfalls overwhelm already strained humanitarian operations.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Afghan mothers and children face worsening hunger crisis, WFP warns

events.title: UAE reports strike near Abu Dhabi nuclear power plant
events.description: The country is investigating the source of the strike, which it calls a "dangerous escalation".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UAE reports strike near Abu Dhabi nuclear power plant

events.title: World News in Brief: UN relief vehicle struck in Ukraine, emergency airdrops in South Sudan, backlash against LGBTIQ+ rights
events.description: The Secretary-General is alarmed that a clearly marked United Nations vehicle was struck twice in Kherson city in Ukraine on 14 May, his spokesperson said in a statement.

events.title: Loneliness: South Korea's silent epidemic
events.description: Half of all households in South Korea's capital Seoul have just one inhabitant. That trend is likely to continue, with more than 40 percent of men and women aged 25 to 49 saying they do not want to get married. Local authorities now consider solitude a serious public health issue. Seoul's municipal government is investing more than €260 million over five years to tackle what it calls an epidemic of loneliness. FRANCE 24's Chloé Borgnon, Junwon Bae and Justin McCurry report.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Loneliness: South Korea's silent epidemic

events.title: Migrants ‘feared for their lives’ as Libyan gunmen fired on rescue ship
events.description: The Libyan coastguard threatened those on board Sea-Watch 5, but it's the NGO ship's captain who is under investigation.

events.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses primary. And, WHO declares Ebola outbreak a global emergency
events.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.

events.title: Ukraine conducts large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding 12 others
events.description: One of Ukraine's largest drone strikes on Russia killed at least four people, including three near Moscow, and wounded a dozen others, local authorities said Sunday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine conducts large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding 12 others

events.title: 'I don't believe the war is coming to an end': Kyiv-based photographer Mark Neville
events.description: A British photographer who lives in Kyiv has told FRANCE 24 how he doesn't believe the war in Ukraine is coming to an end. Mark Neville takes his photographs to tell the real stories of the Ukrainian people as they fight for their country against the Russian invasion. Amid the latest more positive news from the front line, and further signs that Ukraine is finding new ways of fighting back, he says it is best to take things one day at a time, and that while Russian leader Vladimir Putin is obviously under pressure, that does not necessarily mean the end of the conflict is just around the corner. He spoke to us in Perspective.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'I don't believe the war is coming to an end': Kyiv-based photographer Mark Neville

events.title: Blackouts and shortages disrupt healthcare across Cuba
events.description: Hospitals across Cuba are suspending surgeries, struggling to keep lifesaving equipment running and facing severe medicine shortages as blackouts and fuel shortages push the country’s healthcare system deeper into crisis, senior UN officials warned on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Blackouts and shortages disrupt healthcare across Cuba

events.title: LIVE: Security Council meets on Syria transition and humanitarian crisis
events.description: The UN Security Council meets Friday morning in New York to discuss the evolving situation in Syria, as the country navigates a fragile transition after years of conflict and the fall of the Assad regime. Deputy Special Envoy Claudio Cordone and UN relief chief Tom Fletcher are expected to brief members on political and humanitarian developments. Follow our live coverage for updates from the chamber as they happen.

events.title: HIV prevention and treatment services faltering, warns UNAIDS
events.description: Decades of gains in the fight against AIDS are under growing threat as donor funding declines and community-based health services collapse in some of the world’s most vulnerable countries, the head of the joint UN programme on HIV/AIDS warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 HIV prevention and treatment services faltering, warns UNAIDS

events.title: Austin police arrest 3 juveniles allegedly linked to 12 random shootings injuring 4 people
events.description: Austin police arrested two juveniles after 12 random shootings across the city injured four people, struck a fire station and prompted a shelter-in-place.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Austin police arrest 3 juveniles allegedly linked to 12 random shootings injuring 4 people

events.title: ISIS terror leader at large after US strike kills top commander amid rising Africa threat: analyst
events.description: Human intelligence penetrated deep local networks to kill ISIS West Africa commander al-Minuki, described as the group's biggest loss in years.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ISIS terror leader at large after US strike kills top commander amid rising Africa threat: analyst

events.title: Hamas used sexual violence 'deliberately and systematically' on Oct 7, commission report finds
events.description: Investigators say Hamas perpetrators deliberately used sexual and gender-based violence as a weapon during the Oct. 7 massacres in southern Israel.

events.title: US military carries out more strikes against ISIL fighters in Nigeria
events.description: Attacks conducted days after US and Nigeria say they killed deputy ISIL leader.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military carries out more strikes against ISIL fighters in Nigeria

events.title: US, Nigeria carry out fresh strikes on Islamic State group
events.description: The United States and Nigeria carried out air strikes against Islamic State group targets in northeastern Nigeria on Sunday. The region faces the double threat of attacks from jihadist groups and from criminal gangs that carry out kidnappings, including one that resulted in the abduction of 46 people, most of them children, on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US, Nigeria carry out fresh strikes on Islamic State group

events.title: Us-Iran truce under strain amid stalled talks and attacks on US allies
events.description: Washington and Tehran have been swapping proposals in an effort to end the conflict which broke out on February 28. The two sides have held a single round of talks so far amid a fragile ceasefire in place since April 8. The truce has come under strain following a likely-Iranian attack near a UAE power plant. The stalled talks and rising tension are also worrying markets. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle breaks it down for us.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Us-Iran truce under strain amid stalled talks and attacks on US allies

events.title: Indian worker among four killed as Ukraine launches largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year
events.description: Four people were killed in overnight Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia, authorities have said – in what appears to be the largest attack on Moscow in over a year.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Indian worker among four killed as Ukraine launches largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year

events.title: Gunmen kill 10 people in a town in Mexico's Puebla state, authorities say
events.description: Mexican officials say at least 10 people have been killed in an attack by gunmen in the state of Puebla

events.title: Suspected drunk driver kills 2 pedestrians in violent chain-reaction crash on Manhattan's Upper West Side
events.description: A suspected drunk driver allegedly killed two pedestrians and injured others in a violent chain-reaction crash near Amsterdam Avenue in Manhattan Friday.

events.title: Israeli forces storm Gaza-bound aid flotilla off Cyprus
events.description: Organisers of the flotilla say the vessels are boarded in international waters as Turkiye condemns 'piracy'.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Israeli forces storm Gaza-bound aid flotilla off Cyprus

events.title: Mali’s forces target rebel alliance in junta’s fight to keep power
events.description: Army supported by Russian mercenaries launches airstrikes after offensive by coalition of Islamist extremists and Tuareg separatists Mali’s armed forces, supported by Russian mercenaries, have launched airstrikes targeting a rebel alliance of Islamist extremists and Tuareg separatists as the ruling junta struggles to maintain its hold on power in the unstable west African country. Earlier this week warplanes targeted the key northern town of Kidal, which was lost when the rebels launched a surprise offensive across much of Mali in late April. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mali’s forces target rebel alliance in junta’s fight to keep power

events.title: Pianist Jayson Gillham says he never considered apologising for Gaza comments, trial hears
events.description: Federal court case begins for classical pianist who alleges Melbourne Symphony Orchestra unlawfully discriminated due to his views Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A classical pianist who alleges the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra unlawfully discriminated against him because of his views on Israeli forces killing Palestinian journalists says he did not consider apologising for making the statement at the centre of the case. Pianist Jayson Gillham is suing the MSO over a cancelled Melbourne concert he was contracted to perform on 15 August 2024, a cancellation which he claims was an attempt to silence him over his stance on the Gaza conflict. Continue reading...

events.title: Ten dead in Mexico shooting
events.description: Gunmen killed at least 10 people in Mexico's east-central Puebla state, authorities have said.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Ten dead in Mexico shooting

events.title: Protesters light bonfires during public transport strike in Kenya over fuel prices
events.description: Protests have erupted in Nairobi as a nationwide public transport strike begins over rising fuel prices
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Protesters light bonfires during public transport strike in Kenya over fuel prices

events.title: A strong quake in south China kills 2 and triggers evacuation of 7,000
events.description: A 5.2 magnitude earthquake has struck south China's Guangxi region, killing two people and toppling buildings
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ A strong quake in south China kills 2 and triggers evacuation of 7,000

events.title: Man dies after attack by 13-foot great white shark near popular island resort: officials
events.description: A 38-year-old man was killed in a shark attack near Rottnest Island off Australia's western coast, authorities say, urging additional caution in the area.

events.title: LGBTIQ+ face mounting violence and discrimination, warns UN rights chief
events.description: Violence and discrimination against LGBTIQ+ people are widespread, including at school, where 45 per cent of LGBT youth report being bullied.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 LGBTIQ+ face mounting violence and discrimination, warns UN rights chief

events.title: 1 dead and 9 missing after pickup truck falls off bridge in southern China
events.description: One worker has died and nine remained missing after a pickup truck fell of a bridge in southwest China on Saturday, state media reported
events.ticker_label: 💥 1 dead and 9 missing after pickup truck falls off bridge in southern China

events.title: Trump says Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second in command of ISIS globally, killed in US-Nigerian operation
events.description: President Trump announced U.S. and Nigerian forces eliminated Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, whom he identified as the second in command of ISIS globally.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Trump says Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second in command of ISIS globally, killed in US-Nigerian operation

events.title: 'This may be the last time you hear my voice': Political executions surge in Iran since start of war
events.description: Since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February, the UN says it’s verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'This may be the last time you hear my voice': Political executions surge in Iran since start of war

events.title: New Epstein victims turn up in French probe — prosecutor
events.description: France is pursuing its own investigation into the Epstein affair, looking at potential crimes committed in the EU country or involving French nationals tied to the US sex offender.
events.ticker_label: 💥 New Epstein victims turn up in French probe — prosecutor

events.title: French cinema rocked by blacklist threat from biggest producer
events.description: It is a shock threat to Europe's film industry. The head of France's biggest film producer, Canal Plus, is saying it will blacklist 600 film industry professionals who have signed a petition against the right-wing billionaire owner Vincent Bolloré. The announcement was made in Cannes in southern France where the world's movie elite is gathered for the Cannes Film Festival.

events.title: Attacks on schools in Nigeria leave more than 80 children missing, officials say
events.description: Officials say that more than 80 children are missing after suspected militants attacked schools in Nigeria in the past week

events.title: As war displaces millions in DR Congo, new schools offer children hope beyond violence
events.description: Ten-year-old Shadrac Anyazaka does not hesitate when asked about his future. “After finishing my studies, I would like to become President of the Republic one day,” he tells visitors to his school in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As war displaces millions in DR Congo, new schools offer children hope beyond violence

events.title: Somalia at ‘real risk of famine’ as Middle East war fallout continues
events.description: At least six million people in Somalia are going days without enough food, UN aid teams warned on Friday, highlighting that nearly two million of this number are young children “at high risk of illness or death”.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Somalia at ‘real risk of famine’ as Middle East war fallout continues

events.title: Missing American's husband sat on boat for 24 hours after she vanished in Bahamas, friend says
events.description: A friend says Brian Hooker stayed on his sailboat for 24 hours after his wife disappeared in the Bahamas and provided few details to search and rescue crews.

events.title: Maldives military diver dies searching for four Italian divers missing inside underwater cave system
events.description: A Maldivian military diver died from decompression sickness during a perilous mission to recover bodies of four Italian divers trapped in a deep underwater cave.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Maldives military diver dies searching for four Italian divers missing inside underwater cave system

events.title: How worrying is the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo?
events.description: An outbreak in DR Congo has been declared a health emergency, with almost 250 suspected cases reported.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Judge orders Trump administration to return Colombian woman deported to DRC back to the US
events.description: Judge called Adriana Maria Quiroz Zapata’s deportation to the Democratic Republic of Congo ‘likely illegal’ A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to bring a Colombian woman back to the US from the Democratic Republic of Congo , after she was deported to the African country that had refused to accept her. The deportation of Adriana María Quiroz Zapata “was likely illegal”, the US district judge Richard Leon ruled on Wednesday. Continue reading...

events.title: White House prayer rally draws thousands to Washington
events.description: Christians gathered for a day-long prayer festival held in Washington on May 17. The rally, backed by the Trump administration, has drawn criticism for promoting Christian nationalism, undermining the separation of church and state. Americans who travelled from across the country said it's a response to what they see as a decline of religious values, although Trump's approval rating among white Evangelicals has declined.

events.title: Ukraine's Dnipro and Odesa targeted in Russian strikes
events.description: At least a dozen were injured in central and southern Ukraine overnight, local authorities said. The latest strikes come a day after Kyiv launched hundreds of drone strikes on Russian territory over the weekend.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine's Dnipro and Odesa targeted in Russian strikes

events.title: Large-scale Ukrainian drone attack kills three in Moscow region, says Russia
events.description: Ukraine's president says the strikes are a "justified" response to deadly Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Large-scale Ukrainian drone attack kills three in Moscow region, says Russia

events.title: Hezbollah drone strike videos show evolving tactics against Israel
events.description: BBC Verify has examined dozens of videos of fibre-optic drone attacks carried out by the Lebanese armed group.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hezbollah drone strike videos show evolving tactics against Israel

events.title: Taiwan insists it is independent after Trump warning
events.description: Fresh from his summit in Beijing, the US president warned against a formal declaration of independence by the island.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Taiwan insists it is independent after Trump warning

events.title: Mali drone strikes kill at least 10 civilians at wedding
events.description: Mali's military carries out strikes after attacks last month by al-Qaeda-linked fighters and Tuareg separatists.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mali drone strikes kill at least 10 civilians at wedding

events.title: Ukraine launches large-scale drone strikes on Russia
events.description: As peace talks stall, Ukraine launches one of the biggest drone strikes against Russia since the beginning of Russia's full-scale invasion in an attempt to weaken Russia's war machine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine launches large-scale drone strikes on Russia

events.title: Young Kyiv couple killed in a fierce Russian airstrike hoped to start a family, mourners say
events.description: Maryna Homeniuk fled Ukraine after Russia’s full-scale invasion four years ago
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Young Kyiv couple killed in a fierce Russian airstrike hoped to start a family, mourners say

events.title: Israeli military strikes add to civilian suffering in Gaza, West Bank and Lebanon
events.description: Civilians across Gaza, the West Bank and Lebanon continue to face mounting hardship as conflict, displacement and ongoing military operations deepen humanitarian needs and strain already limited aid efforts, according to the latest updates from UN agencies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli military strikes add to civilian suffering in Gaza, West Bank and Lebanon

events.title: Arkansas man allegedly threatened Walmart mass shooting over hantavirus lockdown fears, authorities say
events.description: Arkansas man arrested for allegedly threatening a Walmart mass shooting over a potential hantavirus lockdown, according to the Marion County Sheriff.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Arkansas man allegedly threatened Walmart mass shooting over hantavirus lockdown fears, authorities say

events.title: Massachusetts governor asks US Navy to help retrieve bodies, evidence from sunken fishing vessel

events.title: Peru presidential standoff confirmed after month-long vote count
events.description: The daughter of late former president Alberto Fujimori, Keiko Fujimori will stand against the leftwing Roberto Sanchez in the second round of Peru's presidential elections. Sanchez's place in the contest was confirmed by the country's national election board on May 17 after he gathered over 2 million votes. But his position in the contest is not without controversy. Sanchez was accused of financial crime by the public prosecutor's office and the ultraconservative Rafael Lopez Aliga, who came third in the first round has said he will not recognise the election outcome.

events.title: Weather tracker: Furnace Creek sizzles as snow sweeps Siberia in a week of extremes
events.description: Record heat in North and Central America coincides with egg-sized hailstones in eastern China Extreme weather across several parts of the world this week has brought record-breaking temperatures to Honduras, North America and Indonesia. Honduras smashed its all-time May maximum temperature record earlier this month – only for it to be broken again on 13 May in Choluteca, known as the furnace of Central America. Temperatures climbed to 42.2C (107.9F), surpassing the previous record of 42.1C. With intense heat forecast to persist over the coming weeks, more records are expected to fall. Continue reading...

The US trade representative’s comments to Bloomberg on Friday suggest a breakthrough on selling Nvidia’s advanced H200 chips to China remains far away, Reuters is reporting, despite Nvidia chief Jensen Huang ’s last-minute invitation to Donald Trump ’s Beijing trip this week. This was not a major topic of discussion at the bilateral meeting. We did not talk about chip export controls at the meeting.” First of all, it’s really important for China to have the strait of Hormuz open – no tolling, no military control. That was clear from the meeting, so we welcome that. With respect to Chinese involvement with Iran, our view is the Chinese are being very pragmatic – they don’t want to be on the wrong side of this. They want to see peace in that area, President Trump wants to see peace in that area, so we have a lot of confidence that they will do what they can to limit any kind of material support for Iran.” Continue reading...

events.title: ‘A book that should be read by all Australians’: Clare Wright wins book of the year at the NSW Literary awards
events.description: The historian won $50,000 for her nonfiction book Näku Dhäruk: The Bark Petitions, which judges praised as deeply researched, ‘highly original’ and ‘vividly alive’ A “highly original” nonfiction by Melbourne historian Clare Wright, charting the creation of the Yirrkala Bark Petitions – a seminal moment in Australia’s history of land rights – has won book of the year at the NSW literary awards. The Petitions were landmark documents presented by Yolŋu elders to the Australian parliament in 1963 on painted bark frames, which sought government intervention after a portion of Arnhem Land Reserve was licensed to a French mining company. Though it didn’t halt mining on the land, the petitions led to the first land rights legislation in Australia, the Aboriginal Land Rights (Northern Territory) Act 1976. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘A book that should be read by all Australians’: Clare Wright wins book of the year at the NSW Literary awards

events.title: Romaine calm: live frog found in discount supermarket lettuce bag
events.description: ‘Look, if I was in a French supermarket, I probably would have got a two for one deal on that one,’ woman in WA told national TV Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When a group of housemates in Western Australia bought a pair of lettuces in a sealed plastic bag – reduced for quick sale to $1.15 – they got two other things for which they could not have bargained. One was a live frog reckoned to be the size of a man’s palm. The other, a slot on national television. But despite describing it as “the most random thing” they had encountered upon opening a packet of leafy greens from the supermarket, Laura Jones and Billie Le Pine fronted their interview on ABC news breakfast with a series or ready made one liners. Continue reading...

events.title: Chalmers complains of ‘unhinged scare campaign’ against changes – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Data reveals how Coles and Woolworths switch promotions in sync You’re looking to buy an electric toothbrush. You’re in Woolworths and you see one of the Oral-B kits advertised for sale at $99.50. We understand that people will have strong views about the politics of this but it is the substance of the changes that we are making that matters most and the substance of what we are announcing today: tens of thousands of new homes for Queenslanders including tens of thousands of homes for first home buyers. This is how you make a tangible difference to the housing market in this country, taking difficult decisions, building more homes, making more of those homes available to first home buyers. I’m confident that the substance of what we’re doing is the right decision, taken for the right reasons. Continue reading...

events.title: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
events.description: The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.

events.title: Peru's electoral board confirms June 7 presidential runoff
events.description: Authorities confirmed on Sunday the official results of the first round of the presidential elections in early April, with Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez advancing to the runoff on June 7.

events.title: Spain acquits Shakira of tax fraud
events.description: Spanish tax authorities have been ordered to refund the Colombian singer $55 million euros. However, the singer did plead guilty in a separate case.

events.title: Germany: Chemical industry down, but not out
events.description: Hit by high energy costs and regulation, Germany’s chemical companies are scaling back production at home while expanding abroad. Can the trend be reversed?

events.title: MV Hondius docks in Rotterdam after hantavirus ordeal
events.description: The hantavirus-stricken MV Hondius cruise ship has arrived at its final destination, Rotterdam. The remaining crew on board, including two medics, will disembark the ship.

events.title: Australia orders firms to sell stakes in rare earths miner
events.description: The treasurer ordered China-linked companies to divest of Northern Minerals to protect Australia's interests.

events.title: Thousands gather in Washington for America-themed prayer rally
events.description: Donald Trump and top Cabinet officials addressed the thousands of people gathered at the mass prayer event in Washington.

events.title: German singer Heino pushes back against the far-right AfD
events.description: A far‑right politician in Germany used schlager icon Heino in election advertising without his consent. Heino is now demanding compensation for pain and suffering.

events.title: Are Google and SpaceX plotting space-based data centres?
events.description: Tech giants are increasingly looking beyond Earth to solve one of AI's biggest growing pains: where to put all the computers.

events.title: Six French football clubs qualify for 2027 European competitions
events.description: Football club Paris Saint-Germain lifted the Ligue 1 trophy but then lost 2-1 away to neighbours Paris FC in their last game before the Champions League final against Arsenal. There were plenty of surprises during the French football competition - some bad ones for PSG and some good ones for Marseille. In total, six French clubs will be playing in European competitions next year, like Lens in the Champions League and Rennes in the Conference League.

events.title: China hosts 23rd edition of iconic Great Wall marathon
events.description: Runners from across the globe put their physical and mental stamina to the test on May 16: to conquer the Great Wall of China Marathon. The race that starts and finishes near the Huangyaguan fortress celebrated its 23rd anniversary this year. It is known for being a particularly gruelling course, but one that's worth it. It is even on the Forbes Top 10 List of Marathons worth travelling for.

events.title: Shakira acquitted of tax fraud
events.description: Singer Shakira has been acquitted of tax fraud and should be reimbursed more than 55m euros (£48m), Spain's High Court has ruled, following years of financial legal battles.

events.title: NATO's biggest special forces exercise in Europe takes place as US reduces support for allies
events.description: Some 3,000 elite troops from the US, the UK and 22 other nations are taking part in NATO's biggest special forces exercise in Europe even as Donald Trump reduces support to his allies.

events.title: Aaron Rai becomes first English winner of PGA Championship in over a century
events.description: England's Aaron Rai has claimed his first major championship by winning the US PGA Championship - and becomes the first English winner of the event in more than a century.

events.title: After years of tension, Hungary and Ukraine hold talks on Hungarian minority rights
events.description: Hungary and Ukraine are planning to start high-level consultations on the rights of Ukraine's ethnic Hungarian minority

events.title: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
events.description: A Spanish court has acquitted pop star Shakira in a tax fraud case and ordered the government to return over 55 million euros in wrongly imposed fines

events.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi released from hospital
events.description: Iranian Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has been released from the hospital after more than two weeks

events.title: Peru's electoral board confirms June 7 presidential runoff with Fujimori and Sánchez
events.description: Peruvian electoral authorities have confirmed the results of the first round of presidential elections

events.title: Divers arrive in the Maldives to remap the search for the bodies of 4 Italian divers
events.description: Three Finnish divers have arrived in the Maldives to help search for the bodies of four Italian divers believed to be in an underwater cave

events.title: It's official: Wes Streeting of the Labour Party wants to be Britain's next prime minister
events.description: Wes Streeting's ambition to head the British government was one of the worst-kept secrets in U.K. politics

events.title: Turning silence into stories: Cambodian activist amplifies LGBTQI+ youth voices
events.description: Cambodian activist Panha Theng, a 2025 UN Young Leader for the Sustainable Development Goals, says visibility and safe spaces remain critical for LGBTQI+ youth across Southeast Asia amid continuing stigma and discrimination.

events.title: LIVE UPDATES: UN committee commemorates the 78th anniversary of the Nakba
events.description: The Committee on the Exercise of the Inalienable Rights of the Palestinian People meets Friday at the UN Headquarters to commemorate the 78th anniversary of the Nakba. Senior UN officials, including General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock and Assistant Secretary-General Khaled Khiari, are expected to address the meeting. The event will also feature testimonies from Palestinian families and a tribute to historian of Palestine and the Nakba, Walid Khalidi. Follow our live updates from the meeting as it happens.

events.title: Tyler Robinson asks for more secret hearings after losing fight over cameras in court
events.description: Lawyers for accused Charlie Kirk assassin Tyler Robinson push to seal evidence and block public access to parts of a key Utah court hearing.

events.title: Attorney for man accused of throwing rock at endangered Hawaiian monk seal says client was protecting turtles
events.description: Attorney for Washington man accused of throwing a rock at an endangered Hawaiian monk seal says his client never intended to injure the animal on Maui.

events.title: Taiwan's president says island 'will never be sacrificed or traded' in stern rebuke of China
events.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te vows Taiwan will never be sacrificed or traded, rebuking China and thanking Trump for security cooperation.

events.title: Interrogation video shows trans killer’s calm confession to ambushing parents over gender transition battle
events.description: Newly released video shows transgender killer Mia Bailey confessing to murdering both parents for intervening in gender reassignment surgery decision.

events.title: Teen vanished from home decades ago – now feds hope new image and shifting loyalties reveal truth
events.description: Authorities renew their call for information in the 1980 disappearance of 14-year-old Laureen Rahn from her Manchester, New Hampshire, apartment.

events.title: Becky Hill’s 'disgraceful' comments and book ambitions unraveled Alex Murdaugh's murder conviction: docs
events.description: The South Carolina Supreme Court unanimously overturned Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions, citing clerk Becky Hill's jury interference as unprecedented.

events.title: Spurned spouses cash in as North Carolina’s 'homewrecker' law turns cheating into a courtroom jackpot
events.description: North Carolina's homewrecker laws take center stage as former Sen. Kyrsten Sinema faces an alienation of affection lawsuit over alleged affair.

events.title: Alex Murdaugh defense teases new trial strategy with jury fight, testimony looming
events.description: Prosecutor Creighton Waters and defense attorney Dick Harpootlian preview competing strategies ahead of Alex Murdaugh's retrial in South Carolina.

events.title: Mother recounts horrors of brutal Chinese detention camp where infant son died
events.description: Uyghur mother Mihrigul Tursun recounts years of alleged detention, torture and family separation in China's Xinjiang camps in emotional testimony.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mother recounts horrors of brutal Chinese detention camp where infant son died

events.title: Trump pledges to raise detained pastor's case with Xi Jinping during Beijing visit as family pleads for help
events.description: Pastor Ezra Jin's daughter speaks about her father's detention in China and Trump's pledge to raise his case with Xi Jinping during Beijing visit.
